Rrm4 iCLIP2 crosslink events across stop codon in mitochondrial transcripts

Author

Srimeenakshi Sankaranarayanan

Published

June 12, 2026

Other Formats

  • PDF

In this analysis, I will compare the Rrm4 crosslink signals from iCLIP and iCLIP2 methods across stop codon of mitochondrial transcripts associated with complex I, III and V. Genes encoding proteins involved in mitochondrial complex I and III were obtained from Nina. Whereas, genes encoding ATPase synthase FO F1 complex were obtained from Olgeiser et.al (2019).

Show the code 
# Libraries
library(BSgenome.Umaydis.ENSMBL.UM1) # forged tomato genome
library(ggplot2)
library(BindingSiteFinder)
library(rtracklayer)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(circlize)
library(GenomicFeatures)
library(forcats)
library(tidyr)

library(GenomicRanges)
library(magick)
library(magrittr)
library(gridExtra)
library(IRanges)
library(Biostrings)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggpubr)
library(viridis)
library(reshape2)
library(gprofiler2)
library(ggsci)
library(GetoptLong)
library(tidyHeatmap)

## set the theme for ggplots
my_theme = theme_set(theme_bw() +
          theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 10, colour="black"),
                axis.title = element_text(size=12, colour="black"),
                axis.ticks=element_line(color="black"),
                axis.ticks.length=unit(.15, "cm"),
                panel.border=element_rect(color="black", fill = NA),
                panel.background = element_blank(),
                plot.background = element_blank(),
                legend.text = element_text(size=10),
                legend.position = "bottom",
                aspect.ratio = 1/1)
)
Show the code 
## min-max normalization function
min_max_noramlize <- function(x){
  x_new <- (x-min(x)) / (max(x)-min(x)) 
  return(x_new)
}
Show the code 
## load gene annotation
gnsN = readRDS("~/Documents/Bioinfo_analysis/iCLIP_course/diffRNA_binding/Annotation_preparation/gns.rds")

trl = readRDS("~/Documents/Bioinfo_analysis/iCLIP_course/diffRNA_binding/Annotation_preparation/trl.rds")
Show the code 
## Load mitochondrial genes
complex_I <- read.csv("~/Documents/Bioinfo_analysis/iCLIP_course/iCLIP2_Methods_Stoffel_etal_2023/complex_1.csv")

complex_III <- read.csv("~/Documents/Bioinfo_analysis/iCLIP_course/iCLIP2_Methods_Stoffel_etal_2023/complex_3.csv")

ATPase_F0_F1 <- read.csv("~/Documents/Bioinfo_analysis/iCLIP_course/iCLIP2_Methods_Stoffel_etal_2023/Mito_gene.csv") %>% 
  dplyr::filter(!Complex == "I") %>%
  dplyr::rename(gene_id = "gene_ID")
Show the code 
## import bw files from iCLIP2
my.path.iclip2 = "~/Documents/Bioinfo_analysis/iCLIP_course/datasets/imb_koenig_2021_11_stoffel_iCLIP_ustilago_Rrm4/merged/bw"

# in plus strand
plus_bw_i2 = list.files(my.path.iclip2, pattern="Rrm4G.v3.duprm.plus.bw", full.names = T)
plus_bw_i2 = import(plus_bw_i2, as = "Rle")


# in minus strand
minus_bw_i2 = list.files(my.path.iclip2, pattern="Rrm4G.v3.duprm.minus.bw", full.names = T)
minus_bw_i2 = import(minus_bw_i2, as="Rle")


# make a list
cl_Rle = list(iC2_p = plus_bw_i2,
              iC2_m = minus_bw_i2)
Show the code 
## get the stop codon region for the mito genes
# make a list with mito genes
mito_genes <- list(
  complex_I = complex_I,
  complex_III = complex_III, 
  ATPase_complex = ATPase_F0_F1)

mito_genes_stop_codon <- lapply(mito_genes, function(x){
  # subset the gns for the mito gens
  gnsM <-  gnsN[gnsN$gene_id %in% x$gene_id] %>% 
    sort()
  
  # get the stop codon
  stop <- flank(gnsM, -303,start = FALSE) %>% 
    flank(.,-3)  %>%
    sort()
  #get the last nucleotide of stop codon
  stops = flank(stop,-1, start=FALSE)  %>% 
   sort(.)

  # stop window
  stops = stops+10  %>% 
   sort(.)
  
  # separate stop window into + and - strand
 stop_ls <- list(stops_pl = stops[strand(stops) == "+"]  %>% 
        sort(.),
 stops_min = stops[strand(stops) == "-"]  %>% 
   sort(.)
 )
  
  return(stop_ls) # stop_ls #stops
})

# sanity check
#getSeq(Umaydis,mito_genes_stop_codon$complex_I )
  • complex_I
  • complex_III
  • ATPase_complex
